Benchmarks and supplementary diagnostics

This notebook reproduces the benchmark plots and supplementary diagnostics that rely primarily on cached outputs bundled with the submission.

Pruning sensitivity: runtime vs accuracy

The next chunks reproduce Figure 7 in Section 4.2 of the main text.

In the next two chunks we study the effect of pruning in the dual particle filter by running the algorithm under different pruning levels and comparing both execution time and accuracy as the pruning threshold changes.

This experiment is computationally intensive, since it requires repeated runs of the filter across multiple parameter settings. For convenience, and to reproduce the figures reported in the paper without re-running the full experiment, we recommend importing the precomputed results stored in times.csv and score.csv and using them to generate the plots.

my_labels <- c("    1 node", "  10 nodes", "100 nodes", "  90% mass", "  95% mass", "100% mass")

my_times <- read.csv(cached_results_path("my_times.csv"))
my_times$Pruning <- factor(my_times$Pruning, levels = my_labels, ordered = TRUE)

my_scores <- read.csv(cached_results_path("my_scores.csv"))
my_scores$Pruning <- factor(my_scores$Pruning, levels = my_labels, ordered = TRUE)
my_plot_time <-
  ggplot(data = my_times) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(y = Time, color = Pruning), size = .5, show.legend = TRUE, outlier.size = 1) +
  facet_grid(. ~ factor(Observations)) +
  scale_y_continuous(transform = "log10", limits = c(10, 1000)) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = NULL) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 15), text = element_text(size = 15)) +
  labs(y = "log(Seconds)", x = "Partitions' norm")

my_plot_scores <-
  ggplot(data = my_scores) +
  geom_boxplot(aes(y = Score, color = Pruning), lwd = .5, show.legend = TRUE, outlier.size = 1) +
  facet_grid(. ~ factor(Observations)) +
  scale_color_brewer(palette = "Dark2") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = NULL) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 15), text = element_text(size = 15)) +
  ylim(0, max(my_scores$Score)) +
  labs(y = "Score", x = "Partitions' norm")

my_plot_time

my_plot_scores

Comparing filtering and smoothing methods

The next chunks reproduce Figure 9 in Section 4.4 of the main text.

In the next chunks we compare the accuracy of the different filtering and smoothing approaches considered in the paper. These comparisons rely on extensive simulation runs and are therefore computationally expensive.

To reproduce the corresponding figure in the manuscript without re-running the full simulation study, we recommend importing the precomputed simulation outputs and directly printing the results.

my_data3 <- read.csv(cached_results_path("my_data3.csv"))
my_plot3 <-
  ggplot(data = my_data3, aes(y = Data, x = "", color = factor(Type))) +
  geom_boxplot(outliers = TRUE, show.legend = FALSE, outlier.size = 1.5, coef = 1) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1.5, show.legend = FALSE) +
  scale_x_discrete(breaks = NULL) +
  ylim(0, 3) +
  facet_grid(. ~ factor(Type, levels = c("Independent", "Bootstrap", "Filter", "Oracle Filter", "Smoother", "Oracle Smoother"))) +
  scale_colour_manual(values = c("hotpink", "orchid1", "darkgray", "royalblue1", "tomato", "orange")) +
  theme_light() +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(size = 20), text = element_text(size = 20)) +
  labs(y = "Score", x = "")

my_plot3

Proportion of filtering mixture components

The following chunks return Figure SM.2 in Section SM.4.2 of the supplement.

Here we investigate how the mixing measure concentrates on the coagulations indexing the (dual) filtering distribution – a diagnostic on the concentration of the filtering mixture over the active coagulations The figures show that most of the mass is concentrated on a small subset of the coagulation, thereby justifying the use of the pruning strategy.

size_top_update <- read.csv(cached_results_path("size_top_update.csv"))
size_top_update$time <- as.factor(size_top_update$time)

top_update_relative <- read.csv(cached_results_path("top_update_relative.csv"))
top_update_relative$time <- as.factor(top_update_relative$time)

size_top_propagation <- read.csv(cached_results_path("size_top_propagation.csv"))
size_top_propagation$time <- as.factor(size_top_propagation$time)

top_propagation_relative <- read.csv(cached_results_path("top_propagation_relative.csv"))
top_propagation_relative$time <- as.factor(top_propagation_relative$time)
plot_update_size <-
  ggplot(size_top_update, aes(x = time, y = data, colour = type, group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Time", y = "", title = "Absolute values", subtitle = "Pruning after each update")

plot_update_top <-
  ggplot(top_update_relative, aes(x = time, y = data, colour = type, group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Time", y = "", colour = "", title = "Relative values", subtitle = "Pruning after each update")

plot_prop_size <-
  ggplot(size_top_propagation, aes(x = time, y = data, colour = type, group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Time", y = "", title = "Absolute values", subtitle = "Pruning after each propagation")

plot_prop_top <-
  ggplot(top_propagation_relative, aes(x = time, y = data, colour = type, group = type)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(x = "Time", y = "", colour = "", title = "Relative values", subtitle = "Pruning after each propagation")

plot_update_size

plot_update_top

plot_prop_size

plot_prop_top
